title: Role-Based Agent Teams
concept: Defining agents with specific background stories, goals, and customized toolsets.

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of defining roles in an agent team?

options.text: To give each agent a unique background story
To ensure each agent has specific goals and tools
To make the team look more interesting

exercises.explain: Defining roles allows each agent to have tailored goals and tools, making the team more effective.

cards.title: Why Roles Matter
cards.body: In a team of agents, each agent can have a unique role that helps achieve common goals.

cards.title: What is a Role-Based Agent Team?
cards.body: A role-based agent team consists of agents with specific background stories, goals, and toolsets designed for teamwork.

cards.title: Think of a Play
cards.body: In a play, actors have different roles like hero, villain, and sidekick. Each role is important for the story, just like agents in a team.

cards.title: Examples of Agent Roles
cards.body: An agent may be a 'Strategist' with planning tools, while another is an 'Executor' who carries out tasks.

cards.title: Myth: All Agents are the Same
cards.body: Not all agents are the same! They have different backgrounds and tools tailored to their roles.

cards.title: Roles in Agent Teams
cards.body: See how different agent roles contribute to success.

left.text: Leads the team and makes big decisions.

exercises.prompt: What defines a specific role in an agent team?

options.text: Having a single goal for the entire team
Unique background stories and customized tools

exercises.explain: A specific role is defined by unique background stories and the tools that an agent uses to achieve their goals.

exercises.prompt: All agents in a team should have the same set of tools.
exercises.explain: False! Each agent should have different tools based on their specific roles.

exercises.prompt: An agent may be a 'Strategist' with planning tools, while another is an __________ who carries out tasks.
exercises.sentence: An agent may be a 'Strategist' with planning tools, while another is an Executor who carries out tasks.
exercises.explain: The correct term 'Executor' reflects the specific role and responsibilities defined for that agent in the team.

exercises.prompt: Which agent role focuses on executing tasks?
exercises.explain: The 'Executor' role is specifically designed for carrying out tasks within the team.

exercises.prompt: What can result from having a diverse set of roles in an agent team?

options.text: All agents will perform the same tasks

exercises.explain: Having a diverse set of roles leads to better collaboration, as each agent contributes uniquely based on their specific goals and toolsets.
